hypothesis: Fine-tuning pre-trained ML interatomic potentials (such as eSEN or eqV2) specifically on stress tensor data generated along DFT-converged SSNEB pathways—rather than on bulk equilibrium configurations alone—will disproportionately reduce stress prediction errors and thereby eliminate the symmetry-breaking artifacts that currently force computationally expensive VASP restart settings (e.g., ISYM=0), yielding greater than 5-fold overall speedup relative to pure DFT-SSNEB while maintaining energy barrier accuracy within 0.05 eV.
